page: UpCloud GPU-12xCPU-128GB-1xL40S is a GPU-accelerated server configured with an AMD EPYC 9575F processor featuring 12 vCPUs across 12 physical cores on the x86_64 architecture. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing 10.67 GB of RAM per core. Graphics and parallel computing acceleration are delivered via a single NVIDIA L40S GPU based on the Ada Lovelace architecture, which includes 48 GB of dedicated VRAM. This instance operates on the KVM hypervisor and does not include local storage, requiring external storage attachments for persistent data.

Benchmark data indicates exceptional single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU testing and Geekbench single-core scores placing in the top tier (top 10%). Single-threaded operations such as gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also achieve top-tier results. Multi-core performance, including Geekbench multi-core and Passmark CPU scores, falls within the average range. Memory performance is strong, with uncached large-block read and write bandwidth ranking in the top 25%. For AI workloads, LLM inference testing shows strong performance for text generation using small models (135M) and medium models (7B), while prompt processing and larger model execution (70B) perform at average levels.

From a resource density and cost-efficiency perspective, this server offers a high ratio of memory to CPU cores alongside a high-end accelerator, making it a specialized option for workloads that require substantial GPU memory and strong single-thread CPU performance without needing massive multi-core CPU scaling. The absence of local storage represents a tradeoff, requiring users to factor in external storage performance and costs. This configuration is highly suited for machine learning inference, image processing, text processing, and single-threaded data compression tasks.
description: UpCloud GPU-12xCPU-128GB-1xL40S is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 12 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9575F processor on the x86_64 architecture, paired with 128.0 GB of system memory. Acceleration is provided by a single NVIDIA L40S GPU with 48 GB of VRAM. The server operates on the KVM hypervisor and contains no local storage. Performance benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU capabilities and strong uncached memory bandwidth, though multi-core CPU metrics remain average. LLM inference benchmarks demonstrate strong text generation speeds for small and medium models. Given its high memory-to-core ratio and dedicated GPU, this instance is highly suited for machine learning inference, image processing, and single-threaded data processing workloads.
og_description: UpCloud GPU-12xCPU-128GB-1xL40S is a KVM-based server featuring 12 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA L40S GPU with 48 GB VRAM. It delivers top-tier single-core performance for AI inference and image processing.
meta_description: UpCloud GPU-12xCPU-128GB-1xL40S is a GPU server with 12 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA L40S GPU, optimized for AI inference and image processing.
tagline: A GPU-accelerated server combining top-tier single-core CPU performance with an NVIDIA L40S GPU and 128 GB of system memory.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 9575F processor with 12 vCPUs and 128 GB RAM
NVIDIA L40S GPU with 48 GB VRAM for hardware acceleration
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Strong uncached memory read and write bandwidth
No local storage included, requiring external storage solutions
Best suited for machine learning inference and image processing
